description: Review Workflow milestone — implements the complete review workflow including state transitions, reviewer assignments, review actions, timeline, and review page for Reviewer role.

userStories.title: Submit Idea for Review
userStories.description: As an idea owner, I want to submit my idea for review so that reviewers can evaluate and approve it
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/ideas/:id/submit creates immutable BRD version with sequential version_number
State transitions from 'open' to 'in_review'
PDF generated and stored with pdf_file_path in brd_versions table
Review assignments created for selected reviewers (if any)
Timeline entry created for submission with entry_type='state_change'
Submit message (if provided) creates comment entry in review_timeline_entries
Resubmission creates new BRD version, links old_version_id and new_version_id in timeline entry_type='resubmission'
Brainstorming section locks (chat becomes read-only) after submit
Only 'open' and 'rejected' ideas can be submitted (400 for other states)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § POST /api/ideas/:id/submit — request: {message?: string, reviewer_ids?: uuid[]}, response: {version_number, pdf_url, state}. docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § brd_versions (immutable snapshot), review_assignments, review_timeline_entries (state_change + resubmission types)
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § SubmitArea (message field + reviewer selector) — in Review tab below PDF preview
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: T-1.5.01, T-1.5.05 (state transitions), T-4.7.01 (version creation), T-4.10.01, T-4.10.02 (reviewer assignment), API-SUBMIT.01-04 (API validation). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing the feature (test-first). Each test must include: test ID, layer, description, input, expected output.
Files: services/gateway/apps/ideas/views.py (add submit_idea function), services/gateway/apps/brd/views.py (version creation logic), services/gateway/apps/review/views.py (timeline creation), services/gateway/apps/ideas/serializers.py (SubmitIdeaSerializer), services/gateway/apps/review/models.py (ReviewAssignment, ReviewTimelineEntry mirror models), services/gateway/apps/review/migrations/, tests/test_submit.py
Gotchas: Submit is an atomic multi-table transaction (BRD version + state change + assignments + timeline). Use database transaction.atomic(). Version number must be calculated as MAX(version_number)+1 for the idea. PDF generation is async — store pdf_file_path after PDF service responds. Resubmission logic: if previous submission exists, create resubmission timeline entry with old_version_id and new_version_id.

data_model: brd_versions: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK, version_number int, section_* text (6 fields), pdf_file_path varchar, created_at timestamp — immutable snapshots on submit
review_assignments: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK, reviewer_id uuid FK, assigned_by varchar (submitter/self), assigned_at timestamp, unassigned_at timestamp — tracks reviewer assignments

review_timeline_entries: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK, entry_type varchar (comment/state_change/resubmission), author_id uuid FK, content text, parent_entry_id uuid FK, old_state varchar, new_state varchar, old_version_id uuid FK, new_version_id uuid FK, created_at timestamp — audit trail
ideas: state varchar (open/in_review/accepted/dropped/rejected) — transitions via submit/review actions
api_endpoints: POST /api/ideas/:id/submit — {message?: string, reviewer_ids?: uuid[]} → 200 {version_number, pdf_url, state}. 400 INVALID_STATE for non-open/rejected ideas. 403 ACCESS_DENIED if not owner. 503 PDF_GENERATION_FAILED.
test_cases: T-1.5.01 | Integration | Open → In Review via submit | POST /api/ideas/:id/submit | State = in_review, BRD version created
T-1.5.05 | Integration | Rejected → In Review via resubmit | POST /api/ideas/:id/submit | State = in_review, new BRD version
T-4.7.01 | Integration | Submit creates immutable BRD version | POST /api/ideas:id/submit | New brd_versions row
T-4.10.01 | Integration | Submit with reviewer IDs creates assignments | POST submit with reviewer_ids | review_assignments created
T-4.10.02 | Integration | Submit without reviewers goes to shared queue | POST submit without reviewer_ids | Appears in unassigned list
API-SUBMIT.01 | POST /api/ideas/:id/submit | Success | Valid request | 200 with version data
API-SUBMIT.02 | POST /api/ideas/:id/submit | Invalid state | Idea state = accepted | 400 INVALID_STATE
API-SUBMIT.03 | POST /api/ideas/:id/submit | Access control | Non-owner | 403 ACCESS_DENIED
API-SUBMIT.04 | POST /api/ideas/:id/submit | PDF failure | PDF service down | 503 PDF_GENERATION_FAILED
existing_code: services/gateway/apps/brd/views.py: _create_ai_client() → lazy gRPC import pattern for cross-service calls
services/gateway/apps/ideas/views.py: _require_auth(request) → standard auth check, returns user or None
services/gateway/apps/brd/views.py: BrdDraft mirror model → managed=False, db_table='brd_drafts' pattern for Core-owned tables

userStories.title: Self-Assignment & Unassignment API
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to self-assign and unassign ideas for review so that I can manage my review queue
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/reviews/:id/assign creates review_assignment for current user
POST /api/reviews/:id/unassign sets unassigned_at timestamp
Conflict of interest check: reviewer cannot assign to own idea (400 CONFLICT_OF_INTEREST)
Only Reviewer role can assign (403 for User-only)
Only 'in_review' state ideas can be assigned (400 INVALID_STATE for other states)
Duplicate assignment blocked (400 ALREADY_ASSIGNED)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § POST /api/reviews/:id/assign (self-assign), POST /api/reviews/:id/unassign — both require Reviewer role. docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § review_assignments table with assigned_by='self' for self-assignment
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § ReviewCard — Assign/Unassign buttons on review cards
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: T-10.3.01, T-10.3.02 (assign/unassign), T-10.4.01 (conflict of interest), API-REVIEW.03-05 (API validation). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing.
Files: services/gateway/apps/review/views.py (assign_review, unassign_review functions), services/gateway/apps/review/serializers.py, tests/test_review_assignments.py
Gotchas: Conflict of interest check must compare reviewer_id against both owner_id and co_owner_id. Use unique partial index for active assignments (unassigned_at IS NULL).

data_model: review_assignments: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK, reviewer_id uuid FK, assigned_by varchar (submitter/self), assigned_at timestamp, unassigned_at timestamp — unique constraint on (idea_id, reviewer_id) WHERE unassigned_at IS NULL
ideas: owner_id uuid FK, co_owner_id uuid FK — used for conflict of interest check
api_endpoints: POST /api/reviews/:id/assign — {} → 200 {message}. 400 CONFLICT_OF_INTEREST if reviewer = owner/co-owner. 400 ALREADY_ASSIGNED. 400 INVALID_STATE for non-in_review. 403 if not Reviewer.
POST /api/reviews/:id/unassign — {} → 200 {message}. 404 if not assigned. 403 if not Reviewer.
test_cases: T-10.3.01 | Integration | Self-assignment works | POST /api/reviews:ideaId/assign | 200, assignment created
T-10.3.02 | Integration | Unassign works | POST /api/reviews:ideaId/unassign | 200, unassigned_at set
T-10.4.01 | Integration | Conflict of interest blocked | POST /api/reviews:ideaId/assign on own idea | 400 CONFLICT_OF_INTEREST
API-REVIEW.03 | POST /api/reviews/:id/assign | Conflict of interest | Reviewer = owner | 400 CONFLICT_OF_INTEREST
API-REVIEW.04 | POST /api/reviews/:id/assign | Duplicate | Already assigned | 400 ALREADY_ASSIGNED
API-REVIEW.05 | POST /api/reviews/:id/assign | Role check | User role (not Reviewer) | 403

userStories.title: Review Actions API — Accept, Reject, Drop, Undo
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to accept, reject, drop, or undo review actions so that I can manage the review lifecycle
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/ideas/:id/review/accept transitions state to 'accepted', creates timeline entry
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/reject transitions state to 'rejected', creates timeline entry with mandatory comment
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/drop transitions state to 'dropped', creates timeline entry with mandatory comment
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/undo transitions state back to 'in_review', creates timeline entry with mandatory comment
Undo works for accepted, dropped, and rejected states
Only reviewers can perform these actions (403 for non-reviewers)
Mandatory comment validation for reject, drop, undo (400 COMMENT_REQUIRED)
Invalid state transitions return 400 INVALID_STATE
Timeline entries record old_state and new_state for state_change type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § POST /api/ideas/:id/review/accept (no comment required), POST /api/ideas/:id/review/reject (mandatory comment), POST /api/ideas/:id/review/drop (mandatory comment), POST /api/ideas/:id/review/undo (mandatory comment). docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § review_timeline_entries with entry_type='state_change', old_state and new_state fields
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § Review section — reviewer action buttons below PDF preview
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: T-1.5.02-04, T-1.5.06-09 (state transitions), API-REVIEW.06-09 (API validation). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing.
Files: services/gateway/apps/review/views.py (accept_review, reject_review, drop_review, undo_review functions), services/gateway/apps/review/serializers.py (comment validation), tests/test_review_actions.py
Gotchas: State machine validation — only 'in_review' → accepted/rejected/dropped, only accepted/dropped/rejected → in_review via undo. Multiple reviewers: no consensus required, latest action wins. Timeline entries must be atomic with state change.

data_model: review_timeline_entries: entry_type varchar (comment/state_change/resubmission), old_state varchar, new_state varchar, content text (mandatory for reject/drop/undo) — audit trail
api_endpoints: POST /api/ideas/:id/review/accept — {} → 200 {state}. 400 INVALID_STATE if not in_review. 403 if not Reviewer.
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/reject — {comment: string} → 200 {state}. 400 COMMENT_REQUIRED. 400 INVALID_STATE if not in_review. 403 if not Reviewer.
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/drop — {comment: string} → 200 {state}. 400 COMMENT_REQUIRED. 400 INVALID_STATE if not in_review. 403 if not Reviewer.
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/undo — {comment: string} → 200 {state}. 400 COMMENT_REQUIRED. 400 INVALID_STATE if not accepted/dropped/rejected. 403 if not Reviewer.
test_cases: T-1.5.02 | Integration | In Review → Accepted via reviewer accept | POST /api/ideas:id/review/accept | State = accepted
T-1.5.03 | Integration | In Review → Dropped via reviewer drop | POST /api/ideas:id/review/drop with comment | State = dropped
T-1.5.04 | Integration | In Review → Rejected via reviewer reject | POST /api/ideas:id/review/reject with comment | State = rejected
T-1.5.06 | Integration | Accepted → In Review via undo | POST /api/ideas:id/review/undo with comment | State = in_review
T-1.5.07 | Integration | Dropped → In Review via undo | POST /api/ideas:id/review/undo with comment | State = in_review
T-1.5.08 | Integration | Invalid transition | POST /api/ideas:id/review/accept on open idea | 400 error
T-1.5.09 | Integration | Multiple reviewers: latest action wins | Two reviewers act in sequence | Last action determines state
API-REVIEW.06 | POST /api/ideas/:id/review/accept | Success | Valid in_review idea | 200 with state=accepted
API-REVIEW.07 | POST /api/ideas/:id/review/reject | Missing comment | No comment field | 400 COMMENT_REQUIRED
API-REVIEW.08 | POST /api/ideas/:id/review/drop | Invalid state | Idea state = open | 400 INVALID_STATE
API-REVIEW.09 | POST /api/ideas/:id/review/undo | Access control | User role (not Reviewer) | 403

userStories.title: Review Timeline API — GET Entries & POST Comments
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and comment on the review timeline so that I can track review activity and discuss with reviewers
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline returns chronological list of timeline entries
Timeline includes comment, state_change, and resubmission entry types
Nested replies supported via parent_entry_id
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline creates comment entry
POST supports nested replies via parent_entry_id in request
Comments have author_id and content
State change entries have old_state and new_state
Resubmission entries have old_version_id and new_version_id
Entries ordered by created_at ASC
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline (returns array of entries), POST /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline — request: {content: string, parent_entry_id?: uuid}. docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § review_timeline_entries (all entry types, self-referential parent_entry_id for replies)
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § ReviewTimeline, TimelineEntry, CommentInput — chronological feed with nested replies
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: API-TIMELINE.01-05 (GET/POST timeline, nested replies). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing.
Files: services/gateway/apps/review/views.py (get_timeline, post_timeline_comment functions), services/gateway/apps/review/serializers.py (TimelineEntrySerializer), tests/test_review_timeline.py
Gotchas: Nested replies use parent_entry_id — query must support filtering by idea_id and joining parent entries. State change and resubmission entries are system-generated (author_id may be NULL). Timeline is append-only (no DELETE or UPDATE).

data_model: review_timeline_entries: id uuid PK, idea_id uuid FK, entry_type varchar (comment/state_change/resubmission), author_id uuid FK (NULL for system entries), content text, parent_entry_id uuid FK (for nested replies), old_state varchar, new_state varchar, old_version_id uuid FK, new_version_id uuid FK, created_at timestamp
api_endpoints: GET /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline — → 200 [{id, entry_type, author, content, parent_entry_id, old_state, new_state, old_version_id, new_version_id, created_at}, ...]. 403 if no access. 404 if idea not found.
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline — {content: string, parent_entry_id?: uuid} → 201 {entry}. 400 if content missing. 403 if no access. 404 if parent_entry_id not found.
test_cases: API-TIMELINE.01 | GET /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline | Success | Valid idea | 200 with timeline array
API-TIMELINE.02 | GET /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline | Access control | Non-collaborator | 403
API-TIMELINE.03 | POST /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline | Create comment | {content} | 201 with new entry
API-TIMELINE.04 | POST /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline | Nested reply | {content, parent_entry_id} | 201 with parent link
API-TIMELINE.05 | POST /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline | Validation | Empty content | 400

userStories.title: Review Page List API
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to see categorized lists of ideas for review so that I can manage my review queue
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/reviews returns ideas grouped by category
Categories: assigned_to_me, unassigned, accepted, rejected, dropped
Each category contains array of ideas with review metadata
Only Reviewer role can access (403 for User-only)
Ideas in 'in_review' state appear in assigned_to_me or unassigned
Ideas in final states (accepted, rejected, dropped) appear in respective categories
Response includes idea title, state, owner name, submitted date, assigned reviewers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/reviews — response: {assigned_to_me: [{idea, reviewers}], unassigned: [{idea, reviewers}], accepted: [{idea, reviewers}], rejected: [{idea, reviewers}], dropped: [{idea, reviewers}]}. Requires Reviewer role. docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § review_assignments table to determine assigned vs unassigned
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § Review Page — categorized collapsible lists with ReviewCards
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: T-10.1.01, T-10.2.01 (role check + grouping), API-REVIEW.01-02 (API validation). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing.
Files: services/gateway/apps/review/views.py (list_reviews function), services/gateway/apps/review/serializers.py (ReviewListSerializer), tests/test_review_list.py
Gotchas: Query must join ideas, review_assignments, users (for owner name). Assigned_to_me filtered by reviewer_id=current_user AND unassigned_at IS NULL. Unassigned = no active assignments. Final states (accepted/rejected/dropped) show all ideas regardless of assignment.

data_model: ideas: state varchar (open/in_review/accepted/dropped/rejected), owner_id uuid FK — for filtering by state
review_assignments: idea_id uuid FK, reviewer_id uuid FK, unassigned_at timestamp — active assignments WHERE unassigned_at IS NULL
users: display_name varchar — for owner name in response
api_endpoints: GET /api/reviews — → 200 {assigned_to_me: [{id, title, state, owner_name, submitted_at, reviewers: []}], unassigned: [], accepted: [], rejected: [], dropped: []}. 403 if not Reviewer.
test_cases: T-10.1.01 | Integration | Review page requires reviewer role | GET /api/reviews as regular user | 403
T-10.2.01 | Integration | Ideas grouped correctly | GET /api/reviews | 5 groups: assigned, unassigned, accepted, rejected, dropped
API-REVIEW.01 | GET /api/reviews | Success | Reviewer role | 200 with categorized lists
API-REVIEW.02 | GET /api/reviews | Role check | User role (not Reviewer) | 403

userStories.title: Review Page Layout — Frontend
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a dedicated review page with categorized idea lists so that I can manage my review queue
acceptanceCriteria: Review page accessible at /reviews route
Role-gated: only visible to Reviewer role in navbar
Displays 5 collapsible categories: Assigned to me, Unassigned, Accepted, Rejected, Dropped
Each category shows count in header (e.g., 'Assigned to me (3)')
ReviewCard component used for each idea
Empty state for each category when no ideas
Categories are collapsible with chevron indicator
Default: Assigned and Unassigned expanded, others collapsed
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: frontend/src/pages/review-page.tsx (new route), frontend/src/api/review.ts (API client)
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § Review Page (S3) — categorized collapsible lists. docs/03-design/component-specs.md § ReviewCard (extends IdeaCard with author + date + action button)
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: T-10.2.02, T-10.2.03 (UI rendering), UI-REVIEW.01-02 (UI interaction). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing.
Files: frontend/src/pages/review-page.tsx (new), frontend/src/api/review.ts (new), frontend/src/components/review/ReviewCard.tsx (new), frontend/src/__tests__/review-page.test.tsx (new)
Gotchas: Use TanStack Query for GET /api/reviews. Collapsible pattern: shadcn Collapsible component. ReviewCard should extend IdeaCard with additional props (author_name, submitted_at). Empty state component reused from component-specs.md.

components: ReviewCard — extends IdeaCard with author name, submitted date, action button (Assign/Open)
test_cases: T-10.2.02 | Unit | Categories render with counts | Mock data with 2 assigned, 1 unassigned | Headers show counts
T-10.2.03 | Unit | Collapsible categories | Click category header | Category expands/collapses
UI-REVIEW.01 | E2E | Review page navigation | Click Reviews in navbar | /reviews page loads
UI-REVIEW.02 | E2E | Empty state | No ideas in category | Empty state message shown

frontend/src/api/ideas.ts: API client pattern — credentials: 'include', error handling with status code attachment

userStories.title: Self-Assignment UI
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to assign and unassign myself to ideas so that I can manage my review queue
acceptanceCriteria: Assign button on ReviewCard in Unassigned category
Unassign button on ReviewCard in Assigned to me category
Clicking Assign calls POST /api/reviews/:id/assign and moves idea to Assigned category
Clicking Unassign calls POST /api/reviews/:id/unassign and moves idea to Unassigned category
Conflict of interest handled: Assign button disabled with tooltip 'Cannot review own idea'
Loading state on buttons during API call
Error toast on failure with Retry button
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: frontend/src/api/review.ts (assignReview, unassignReview functions)
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § ReviewCard — Assign/Unassign buttons, conflict of interest display
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: UI-ASSIGN.01-03 (UI interaction). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing.
Files: frontend/src/components/review/ReviewCard.tsx (add buttons), frontend/src/api/review.ts (add functions), frontend/src/__tests__/self-assignment.test.tsx (new)
Gotchas: Conflict of interest check: compare current user ID with idea.owner_id and idea.co_owner_id. Use TanStack Query useMutation for assign/unassign. Invalidate 'reviews' query key on success to refetch and update categories.

components: ReviewCard — Assign (primary button) / Unassign (secondary button), disabled state + tooltip for conflict of interest
test_cases: UI-ASSIGN.01 | Unit | Assign button works | Click Assign on unassigned idea | API called, idea moves to assigned category
UI-ASSIGN.02 | Unit | Unassign button works | Click Unassign on assigned idea | API called, idea moves to unassigned category
UI-ASSIGN.03 | Unit | Conflict of interest | Reviewer = owner | Assign button disabled with tooltip

userStories.title: Review Section Below Fold
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the review section below the brainstorming section so that I can view review activity and document versions
acceptanceCriteria: Review section renders below brainstorming section (below fold)
Top area shows: PDF preview thumbnail, idea title, assigned reviewers, state badge
Timeline component renders chronologically ordered entries
Auto-scroll to review section when idea state is 'in_review', 'accepted', or 'dropped'
Auto-scroll to brainstorming when state is 'rejected'
Section visibility: hidden until first submission, then always visible
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:

ReviewTimeline — chronological feed with 3 entry types (comment, state_change, resubmission)

userStories.title: Review Timeline UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and interact with the review timeline so that I can track review activity and add comments
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline entries render chronologically (oldest first)
Comment entries show avatar, author name, timestamp, content
State change entries show system icon, transition text (e.g., 'Submitted for review'), timestamp
Resubmission entries show version links (download buttons for v1 and v2)
Nested replies indented 24px from parent
Reply button on comments to create nested reply
CommentInput at bottom of timeline for top-level comments
POST /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline on submit
Real-time updates via WebSocket (new entries appear without refresh)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: frontend/src/api/review.ts (fetchTimeline, postComment functions)

Gotchas: Entry types: comment (user content), state_change (system-generated, italic text), resubmission (version download links). Nested replies use parent_entry_id. WebSocket event listener for 'ws:timeline_update' CustomEvent to invalidate query.

api_endpoints: GET /api/ideas/:id/review/timeline — → 200 [{id, entry_type, author, content, parent_entry_id, old_state, new_state, old_version_id, new_version_id, created_at}]
components: TimelineEntry — handles 3 types: comment (avatar + name + content), state_change (system icon + text), resubmission (version links)
CommentInput — textarea + send button, supports parent_entry_id for replies

userStories.title: Submit Flow UI — SubmitArea in Review Tab
userStories.description: As an idea owner, I want to submit my idea with optional message and reviewer selection so that I can request review
acceptanceCriteria: SubmitArea renders in Review tab below PDF preview when state is 'open' or 'rejected'
Message textarea (optional) for submit comment
Reviewer multi-select dropdown (optional) with user search
Submit button calls POST /api/ideas/:id/submit with message and reviewer_ids
Loading state during submission
Success: toast 'Idea submitted', state updates to 'in_review', SubmitArea hides
Error: toast with error message and Retry button
Resubmission flow: same UI appears when state is 'rejected'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: frontend/src/api/ideas.ts (submitIdea function)
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § SubmitArea (component-inventory.md) — message field + reviewer selector + submit button. docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § Review tab submit area
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § Test IDs: UI-SUBMIT.01-03 (UI interaction). Ralph writes these tests BEFORE implementing.
Files: frontend/src/components/workspace/ReviewTab.tsx (add SubmitArea), frontend/src/components/review/SubmitArea.tsx (new), frontend/src/api/ideas.ts (add submitIdea function), frontend/src/__tests__/submit-area.test.tsx (new)
Gotchas: Reviewer selector must fetch users with Reviewer role (GET /api/users?role=reviewer). SubmitArea only visible when state='open' OR state='rejected' (resubmission). After submit success, invalidate BRD query to show new version.

api_endpoints: POST /api/ideas/:id/submit — {message?: string, reviewer_ids?: uuid[]} → 200 {version_number, pdf_url, state}
components: shadcn Select — multi-select pattern with search filtering
test_cases: UI-SUBMIT.01 | Unit | Submit with message and reviewers | Fill message, select 2 reviewers, click Submit | POST called with correct payload
UI-SUBMIT.02 | Unit | Submit without optional fields | Click Submit immediately | POST called with empty message and reviewer_ids
UI-SUBMIT.03 | Unit | Submit error handling | API returns 400 | Error toast shown with Retry button
